title: epipactis atropurpurea subvar. virescens
intro: Epipactis atropurpurea subvar. virescens, also known as the green form of the Helleborine orchid, is a rare and unique type of flowering plant that belongs to the orchid family. Traditionally, the Helleborine orchid has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation, treating respiratory issues, and supporting immune function. The herbal preparations of Epipactis atropurpurea subvar. virescens typically involve drying the plant material and using it in teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
